tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: Paralyzed man regains his voice through groundbreaking brain implant
stories.summary: Casey Harrell used a brain implant to speak and work independently from home for two years, translating his thoughts into words and computer commands.

stories.headline: 40-year-old goalkeeper's tears of joy as Cape Verde stuns Spain
stories.summary: Goalkeeper Vozinha wept after Cape Verde's historic 0-0 World Cup draw with Spain, his tears honoring his grandparents and tiny nation's pride.

stories.headline: Neighbors race toward burning home and heroically save elderly woman
stories.summary: Fort Wayne neighbors rushed into a burning house and rescued an elderly woman trapped inside, embodying their neighborhood's motto of neighbors helping neighbors.

stories.headline: Loggerhead turtles surge back to Cabo Verde beaches in astonishing recovery
stories.summary: Loggerhead turtle nesting on Boa Vista's beaches surged from 5-10 females per night to 30-40, an 80-fold increase over 27 years of conservation.

stories.headline: One mom's Facebook post fills a pantry, proves neighbors still show up
stories.summary: Jacquelyn Koester's Facebook post sparked Hobe Sound neighbors to stock a Little Free Pantry, with one person driving from Jupiter for weekly groceries.

stories.headline: Scientists 3D-print a living human ear with cells that organize and thrive
stories.summary: Swiss researchers achieved a breakthrough printing a human ear with living cells inside that survived and formed networks within days.

stories.headline: Hawaii nonprofit celebrates restoring 10,000 corals and inspiring the next generation
stories.summary: Kuleana Coral Restoration reached 10,000 restored corals on Oahu's Waianae Coast after six years, now installing two coral nursery tables as living classrooms.

stories.headline: Senior artists hide kindness rocks for children across their town
stories.summary: Glen Carbon's Senior Center arts and crafts class painted kindness rocks for children to discover during the village's 100th Homecoming celebration.

stories.headline: Dam removal brings 750 'functionally extinct' mussels home to Virginia river
stories.summary: Volunteers reintroduced 750 alewife floater mussels to Virginia's South Anna River just two years after the Ashland Mill Dam removal opened their path home.

stories.headline: Gene therapy repairs muscle-wasting disease using patients' own cells
stories.summary: Peking University researchers used LEAPER 2.0 gene therapy to repair Duchenne muscular dystrophy in human patients, restoring functional dystrophin protein.

stories.headline: Rare marsupials return to Australian outback after decades of absence
stories.summary: After a decade-long rewilding effort, burrowing bettongs were released back to Sturt National Park in far north-west NSW, marking their return after decades of extinction.

stories.headline: Village pub transforms shabby bus stop into a charming little library
stories.summary: The Woolpack Inn in Kent transformed a shabby bus shelter into a charming library with a chandelier, receiving an anonymous £100 donation for upkeep.

stories.headline: Korean inventors create water purifier powered entirely by shaking your hand
stories.summary: Battery-free water purifier kills bacteria and viruses with just hand-shaking, costing under $25 to produce.

stories.headline: Mangrove forests are recovering worldwide, offering climate hope to coastal communities
stories.summary: Mangrove forests have rebounded globally over the past 16 years, with gains now exceeding losses and net decline over four decades reduced to just 1%.

stories.headline: Baby sturgeon thriving in Great Lakes after first reintroduction effort
stories.summary: A collaborative program released juvenile lake sturgeon in Ohio's Maumee River annually since 2018, tracking them thriving with a long-term goal of 1,500 spawning adults.
